retrieved.title: Web services
retrieved.text:
The University of Vienna offers a variety of tools for organising your studies. With your [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) all services are available. On this page your will find information about the most important functions of the web services that will accompany you from the first semester onwards:

- [u:space](#c242229)
- [u:find](#c242234)
- [Moodle](#c242239)
- [IT Services for students](#c242247)

u:space, the portal for the organisation of your studies, offers central access to all services that you will require during your studies.

- Apply for admission to a degree programme
- Order your u:card
- Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
- Register for/de-register from courses and examinations
- Check grades and academic progress in the record of examinations
- Download and print your study documents (transcript of records, confirmation of enrolment, student record sheet, etc.)
- [Room details](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/rauminfo) (room type, equipment, capacity, location of the rooms)

For information about registration and the application for admission to a degree programme, please watch our [video tutorials](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/videos) in u:space.

In u:find you can find all courses offered at the University of Vienna (organised according to semesters), as well as all university departments, units and employees.

- Search for courses, examinations, persons or organisations
- Check course and examination dates
- Register for courses and examinations (you will be forwarded to u:space)
- Calendar export
- Building plans of the University of Vienna via Google Maps

retrieved.title: Attending courses out of interest
heading_path: Your steps in the admission procedure
retrieved.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) withing the application period. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Log into u:space to complete the registration.
3. At "non-degree programme" you will find a link for the [Statistical Data Confirmation (UHStat1)](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/statistische-daten). Fill in the questionnaire and choose "Check and submit".

#### Registration in u:space

1. Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/2148) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
2. Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
3. Pay the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/). The admission is completed as soon as the prescribed fee is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can order your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).

Register for the courses you want to attend. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

retrieved.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) within the application period. Log into u:space and complete your registration. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at your home university. You need the current student record sheet of your home university.

Submit all required documents as PDF files via the [Service Desk of the University of Vienna](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/744).

You are exempted from pre-approval if one of the following conditions applies:

- You are admitted to a Teacher Education Programme at the University of Applied Arts Vienna or the Academy of Fine Arts Vienna.
- You are admitted to a degree programme that was jointly established by the University of Vienna and your home university.
- You are admitted to a degree programme in cooperation with the University of Vienna ("Kooperationsstudium").

retrieved.title: Planning your semester
retrieved.text: / - Step 1: Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee At the beginning of the semester, you have to pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee. You can check whether your payment was successful in the study overview on u:space. Once you have paid your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee, you are admitted for the current semester and can register for courses. [Information about tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) - Step 2: Validate your u:card As soon as you have paid your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee, you can validate your u:card – the University of Vienna’s student ID card – at one of the u:card terminals. Your u:card is then valid for the duration of the current semester. [u:card website](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en/) - Step 3: Plan your schedule The website on the [University of Vienna's range of degree programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) is a good starting point for planning your schedule. In addition to information about your degree programme, you can also find a link to the curriculum there. The curriculum outlines what achievements you have to complete during your degree programme. With the help of the [semester planner in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/semesterplaner), you can plan your courses and exams for the current semester and create your personal schedule. retrieved.title: Planning your semester
retrieved.text:
At the University of Vienna, you do not get a ready-made schedule, telling you what courses to complete each semester. Rather, you have to plan your semester yourself.
The most important tools for doing so are the student platform u:space and the course directory u:find.

[Information about u:space and u:find](/en/web-services-uspace-ufind-moodle-webmail/)

retrieved.title: Why study at the University of Vienna?
retrieved.text: Image 1 of 1 The main building of the University of Vienna houses several institutes, such as the Institute of History, as well as central service facilities, such as the University Library. © University of Vienna / Alex Schuppich The arcade courtyard can be found inside the main building: Here you can learn in good weather or just enjoy the sun. © University of Vienna / Barbara Mair The largest lecture hall in the main building of the University of Vienna is the Audimax, which offers more than 750 seats in the hall and on the balcony. © University of Vienna / Franz Pfluegl You can also learn and work well in other locations of the University Library - the Departmental Libraries - for example in the Education, Linguistics and Comparative Literature Library in Sensengasse 3a. © University of Vienna / Barbara Mair A location of the University of Vienna near the main building is the NIG - the New Institute Building. Many University Departments are located here like the Department of Cultural and Social Anthropology or of Philosophy. © University of Vienna / Peter Wienerroither Another location of the University of Vienna is the Campus. Here you will find, for example, the Department of Art History or of Romance Studies. With its many green courtyards, the Campus offers space for rest and relaxation in the middle of the city.

retrieved.title: Studying and Living - Who can help me with ...?
heading_path: Services of the University of Vienna
retrieved.text: There are many services at the University of Vienna that provide help during your studies. Some of them are listed here: - [Admission Office](/en/): All administrative matters regarding the admission and continuation of degree programmes at the University of Vienna are solved here. - [Directorate of studies - Studienprogrammleitung (SPL)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/): The director of studies plans the teaching offered in a particular degree programme, makes decisions in matters concerning study law and study organisation. - [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/): The SSC/SSS of each degree programme organises and coordinates the teaching and the exams offered in each degree programme. At the SSC/SSS office you get information about the contents of a degree programme, help in planning the semester and information for completing a degree programme. - [Center for Doctoral Studies](http://doktorat.univie.ac.at/en/): all info about Doctoral/PhD Programmes at the University of Vienna - [Postgraduate Center](https://www.postgraduatecenter.at/en/): The Postgraduate Center of the University of Vienna covers all affairs concerning postgraduate programmes. - [Vienna University Computer Center](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en): The Computer Center is responsible for all technical matters of the University of Vienna such as free WiFi, computer rooms, your u:account, the [u:book](https://ubook.at/) sales etc. - [University Library](http://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/english/) - [Center for Teaching and Learning (CTL)](/en/): The CTL offers mentoring programmes for writing scientific papers and many other interesting lectures/info talks. An overview about the university communities is available here: - [#univie Communities](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/univie-communities/) - [Social Media Hub](https://socialmedia.univie.ac.at/) - up-to-date social media

# Student Space: room for students

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

© Alexander / Arnberger

© derknopfdruecker.com

The key objectives of Student Spaces are to shape the University as a living space together with and for students, improve the places of studying and research and create more space for communication.

This website provides information about the Student Space projects. Here, you can see where Student Spaces have been established particularly for students. Additionally, you will gain some insight into the most recent (building) history and development of the University.

The established Student Spaces are available for students as places for studying and recreation. The additional spaces are intended to make the time spent at the University of Vienna more comfortable and to support students in their everyday university lives.

The [opening hours of the respective locations](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/opening-hours-and-locations) apply also to the student spaces.

Monday to Friday 6 a.m. – 10 p.m.

retrieved.title: How do I organise my studies?
retrieved.text:
Please follow the Guidelines for Good Scientific Practice from the beginning of your studies until the graduation. You find useful information here:

<https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/infos-zum-studienrecht/sicherung-der-guten-wissenschaftlichen-praxis/plagiat/> (in German)

[oeawi.at/en/guidelines/](https://oeawi.at/en/guidelines/)

- [Regulation for pregnant students and nursing mothers](/en/pregnant-students-and-nursing-mothers/)

#### Student participation

The Austrian National Union of Students represents all students studying at Austrian universities on both the federal level, and the level of university and studies.

- [Österreichische HochschülerInnenschaft (ÖH)](https://www.oeh.ac.at/) - Austrian National Union of Students
- [ÖH Uni Wien](http://www.oeh.univie.ac.at/) (Austrian National Union of Students at the University of Vienna)
- [Students' representatives at the University of Vienna](http://www.oeh.univie.ac.at/fakultaets-und-studienvertretungen)
- Union of Students Act [(HochschülerInnenschaftsgesetz)](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20008892)

The Unit for Quality Assurance carries out course evaluations and graduate surveys to improve teaching at the University of Vienna. Students are welcome to voice their opinions.

- [Course evaluation](https://www.qs.univie.ac.at/evaluationen/lehrveranstaltungen/)
- [Graduate survey](https://www.qs.univie.ac.at/analysen/studienabschlussbefragung/)
- [Unit for Quality Assurance](https://www.qs.univie.ac.at/)

#### Student mobility

The International Office helps you plan and realise stays abroad during your studies in the framework of mobility programmes.

- [International Office](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/study-abroad-with-erasmus-and-co)

#### Diversity

Diversity enriches our community life by opening up different ways of understanding, thinking and acting. The perception, recognition and utilisation of diversity are therefore key fundamental values of the University of Vienna.

- [Dimensions of diversity](https://gleichstellung.univie.ac.at/en/diversity)
- [Culture & Equality](http://personalwesen.univie.ac.at/gleichstellung-diversitaet/)
- [Contact persons](https://gleichstellung.univie.ac.at/en/prevention-counselling/counselling-office)
- [Accessible studies](/en/accessible-studies/)
- [Accessibility at the University of Vienna](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/)

reranked.title: Web services
reranked.text:
The University of Vienna offers a variety of tools for organising your studies. With your [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) all services are available. On this page your will find information about the most important functions of the web services that will accompany you from the first semester onwards:

- [u:space](#c242229)
- [u:find](#c242234)
- [Moodle](#c242239)
- [IT Services for students](#c242247)

u:space, the portal for the organisation of your studies, offers central access to all services that you will require during your studies.

- Apply for admission to a degree programme
- Order your u:card
- Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
- Register for/de-register from courses and examinations
- Check grades and academic progress in the record of examinations
- Download and print your study documents (transcript of records, confirmation of enrolment, student record sheet, etc.)
- [Room details](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/rauminfo) (room type, equipment, capacity, location of the rooms)

For information about registration and the application for admission to a degree programme, please watch our [video tutorials](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/videos) in u:space.

In u:find you can find all courses offered at the University of Vienna (organised according to semesters), as well as all university departments, units and employees.

- Search for courses, examinations, persons or organisations
- Check course and examination dates
- Register for courses and examinations (you will be forwarded to u:space)
- Calendar export
- Building plans of the University of Vienna via Google Maps

reranked.title: Attending courses out of interest
heading_path: Your steps in the admission procedure
reranked.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) withing the application period. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Log into u:space to complete the registration.
3. At "non-degree programme" you will find a link for the [Statistical Data Confirmation (UHStat1)](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/statistische-daten). Fill in the questionnaire and choose "Check and submit".

#### Registration in u:space

1. Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/2148) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
2. Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
3. Pay the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/). The admission is completed as soon as the prescribed fee is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can order your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).

Register for the courses you want to attend. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

reranked.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) within the application period. Log into u:space and complete your registration. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at your home university. You need the current student record sheet of your home university.

Submit all required documents as PDF files via the [Service Desk of the University of Vienna](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/744).

You are exempted from pre-approval if one of the following conditions applies:

- You are admitted to a Teacher Education Programme at the University of Applied Arts Vienna or the Academy of Fine Arts Vienna.
- You are admitted to a degree programme that was jointly established by the University of Vienna and your home university.
- You are admitted to a degree programme in cooperation with the University of Vienna ("Kooperationsstudium").

# Student Space: room for students

© Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

© Alexander / Arnberger

© derknopfdruecker.com

The key objectives of Student Spaces are to shape the University as a living space together with and for students, improve the places of studying and research and create more space for communication.

This website provides information about the Student Space projects. Here, you can see where Student Spaces have been established particularly for students. Additionally, you will gain some insight into the most recent (building) history and development of the University.

The established Student Spaces are available for students as places for studying and recreation. The additional spaces are intended to make the time spent at the University of Vienna more comfortable and to support students in their everyday university lives.

The [opening hours of the respective locations](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/opening-hours-and-locations) apply also to the student spaces.

Monday to Friday 6 a.m. – 10 p.m.

The Austrian National Union of Students represents all students studying at Austrian universities on both the federal level, and the level of university and studies.

- [Österreichische HochschülerInnenschaft (ÖH)](https://www.oeh.ac.at/) - Austrian National Union of Students
- [ÖH Uni Wien](http://www.oeh.univie.ac.at/) (Austrian National Union of Students at the University of Vienna)
- [Students' representatives at the University of Vienna](http://www.oeh.univie.ac.at/fakultaets-und-studienvertretungen)
- Union of Students Act [(HochschülerInnenschaftsgesetz)](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20008892)

The Unit for Quality Assurance carries out course evaluations and graduate surveys to improve teaching at the University of Vienna. Students are welcome to voice their opinions.

- [Course evaluation](https://www.qs.univie.ac.at/evaluationen/lehrveranstaltungen/)
- [Graduate survey](https://www.qs.univie.ac.at/analysen/studienabschlussbefragung/)
- [Unit for Quality Assurance](https://www.qs.univie.ac.at/)

#### Student mobility

The International Office helps you plan and realise stays abroad during your studies in the framework of mobility programmes.

- [International Office](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/study-abroad-with-erasmus-and-co)

#### Diversity

Diversity enriches our community life by opening up different ways of understanding, thinking and acting. The perception, recognition and utilisation of diversity are therefore key fundamental values of the University of Vienna.

- [Dimensions of diversity](https://gleichstellung.univie.ac.at/en/diversity)
- [Culture & Equality](http://personalwesen.univie.ac.at/gleichstellung-diversitaet/)
- [Contact persons](https://gleichstellung.univie.ac.at/en/prevention-counselling/counselling-office)
- [Accessible studies](/en/accessible-studies/)
- [Accessibility at the University of Vienna](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/)

selected.title: Web services
selected.text:
The University of Vienna offers a variety of tools for organising your studies. With your [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) all services are available. On this page your will find information about the most important functions of the web services that will accompany you from the first semester onwards:

- [u:space](#c242229)
- [u:find](#c242234)
- [Moodle](#c242239)
- [IT Services for students](#c242247)

u:space, the portal for the organisation of your studies, offers central access to all services that you will require during your studies.

- Apply for admission to a degree programme
- Order your u:card
- Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
- Register for/de-register from courses and examinations
- Check grades and academic progress in the record of examinations
- Download and print your study documents (transcript of records, confirmation of enrolment, student record sheet, etc.)
- [Room details](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/rauminfo) (room type, equipment, capacity, location of the rooms)

For information about registration and the application for admission to a degree programme, please watch our [video tutorials](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/videos) in u:space.

In u:find you can find all courses offered at the University of Vienna (organised according to semesters), as well as all university departments, units and employees.

- Search for courses, examinations, persons or organisations
- Check course and examination dates
- Register for courses and examinations (you will be forwarded to u:space)
- Calendar export
- Building plans of the University of Vienna via Google Maps

selected.title: Attending courses out of interest
heading_path: Your steps in the admission procedure
selected.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) withing the application period. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Log into u:space to complete the registration.
3. At "non-degree programme" you will find a link for the [Statistical Data Confirmation (UHStat1)](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/statistische-daten). Fill in the questionnaire and choose "Check and submit".

#### Registration in u:space

1. Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/2148) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
2. Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
3. Pay the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/). The admission is completed as soon as the prescribed fee is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can order your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).

Register for the courses you want to attend. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

selected.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
selected.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) within the application period. Log into u:space and complete your registration. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at your home university. You need the current student record sheet of your home university.

Submit all required documents as PDF files via the [Service Desk of the University of Vienna](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/744).

You are exempted from pre-approval if one of the following conditions applies:

- You are admitted to a Teacher Education Programme at the University of Applied Arts Vienna or the Academy of Fine Arts Vienna.
- You are admitted to a degree programme that was jointly established by the University of Vienna and your home university.
- You are admitted to a degree programme in cooperation with the University of Vienna ("Kooperationsstudium").
